commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: Hence the Hebrews, who groaned in the works of Egypt, attained the grace of the just, and those “who ate bread with mourning and fear” were supplied with spiritual good. The Egyptians, on the other hand, who in their service to a detestable king carried out such works with joy, received no favor.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Died in the year of the world 2494. His successor, Amen phis, treading in his footsteps, was drowned 19 years afterwards. (Usher)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
